The Volunteer Spotlight is on Edith Ritchie!.......Take
Edith and her family enjoy living in Dartmouth
where its family-oriented community and vibrant
history were definite draws. Edith remains active in
Dartmouth life not only through her involvement at
born in St. John, the museum, but also in Port Wallis United Church,
New Brunswick. and the Canadian Federation of University Women.

Edith is a well-rounded member of our community
having obtained a degree in science, with an honours
in chemistry from Mount Allison. While in Sackville
N.B., Edith, not only gained knowledge but also a
Edith and members of the Ritchie Family, first landed
in Dartmouth in 1998. It was at this time that Edith
was looking for new interests and ways to give back
to her new community. She began volunteering with
the Shubencadie Canal Commission at the Fairbanks
Interpretation Center in the area of school program-
ming. Throughout her volunteer time with us, Edith
has been active at the museum front desk and has
been of great help with research and exhibit prepara-
tion. All of this museum work has given her a keen
